title: rosa × dumetorum subvar. vaulxiana
intro: Rosa × dumetorum subvar. vaulxiana, also known as Vaulx's wild rose, is a rare and lesser-known variety of wild rose, a hybrid species of Rosa dumetorum and Rosa gallica. The Vaulxiana subvariety has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Its health benefits include treating digestive issues, reducing anxiety and stress, and providing relief from menstrual cramps and other menstrual-related symptoms. Herbal preparations of Vaulxiana, such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
